summary: Drag-mode extension landed cleanly. constructionSystem.js gained 7 new private methods (_isDragMode/_beginDrag/_onDragMove/_onDragUp/_hitTestAnchors/_cancelDrag/_endDrag) plus a single mode-branch in _renderGhost. Click path preserved as the `else` branch — byte-equivalent to commit 451e7a4. bridgeBlueprint.js gained `mode: 'drag'` on BRIDGE_MESQUITE_BLUEPRINT. quests.js untouched. bridge_built observation event continuity preserved — drag path funnels into the same tryPlace → completeBuild chain as click. Public API (mount/unmount/tryPlace/completeBuild/isComplete/getPlacedSlotIds) unchanged.
review_method: Native code-quality-reviewer dispatch SKIPPED per session-scoped protocol. Orchestrator ran the verification checklist directly.

low.summary: No second blueprint exists yet to provide a functional witness for click-mode backwards compatibility. Worker correctly noted this — backwards compat is witnessed STRUCTURALLY (the click branch in _renderGhost is preserved verbatim and is the only branch reached when mode !== 'drag') but functional witness is gated on a future second blueprint adopting 'click' mode (or absent mode field). Acceptable given the abstraction's first-consumer position.
Worker chose to extend unmount() to cancel in-flight drag so scene-level pointer listeners never leak. Defensive call. Documented in receipt notes; not in the original spec but the right behavior.

Drag path delegates anchor-validity to tryPlace() rather than re-implementing it — the spec required this and the worker honored it. _hitTestAnchors does coarse spatial filtering on slot.x/y/w/h, then tryPlace runs the full validation path (existing check). No logic duplication.

build.duration: 12.30s (consolidated build with both parallel workers' changes)

quests_js_untouched.interpretation: CRITICAL hard-rule honored — quest steps untouched

bridge_built_event_continuity.system_emission: constructionSystem.js completeBuild() reads completion.observation and pushes to state.observations (unchanged)
bridge_built_event_continuity.drag_path_routes_through_completeBuild: Yes — _onDragUp on valid anchor calls tryPlace(slot.id) which on completion calls onAllPlaced and then completeBuild — same chain as click path
bridge_built_event_continuity.interpretation: CRITICAL hard-rule honored — event name + shape preserved

click_path_byte_equivalence.method: Inspect _renderGhost — drag branch is the if-condition, click branch is the else (line `obj.on('pointerdown', () => { this.tryPlace(slot.id); });`)
click_path_byte_equivalence.byte_equivalence_witness: Removing the 7 new drag methods + the mode-branch + the mode line in BRIDGE_MESQUITE_BLUEPRINT yields the original 451e7a4 file byte-equal

saved_memory_systems_not_quests_honored.drag_mode_is_reusable: Yes — any future blueprint can opt in via mode: 'drag'. Bridge is the first consumer, not the only one.

next_action_for_orchestrator: Mark phaser-bridge-construction complete in state.json. Commit alongside phaser-dry-wash-scene (same parallel group). Pod's last agent (phaser-bridge-quest-glue) is now unblocked — it's a verification-first fan-in with a hard 50-LOC cap; most likely outcome is 'no edits needed, here is the traced event chain'.
